Software Installation

PICO is incorporated into the Acro software repository, which supports the integration of PICO with a variety of related software packages (particularly for the PICO MIP solver). This section summarizes how PICO can be downloaded and installed. See the Acro User Guide for a more complete description of this process.

Downloading PICO

The Acro CVS repository is organized into different modules that include different subsets of the Acro packages. The Acro module that only contains PICO is acro-pico, which can be downloaded as a compressed tarfile from http://software.sandia.gov/Acro/.

Alternatively, the acro-pico module can be checked out from the Acro CVS (Concurrent Version System) repository. The CVS respository for Acro can be accessed by executing 

    cvs -d :ext:${userid}@software.sandia.gov:/space/CVS-Acro checkout acro-pico
This requires the use of an account (userid) on software.sandia.gov. Accounts on this machine are generally restricted to Sandians and academic collaborators working with Sandians. The scripts cvs.a and ssh.cvs can be used to encapsulate the access to this repository in a convenient manner; these scripts can be downloaded from http://software.sandia.gov/Acro/.

Building PICO

After the Acro software has been downloaded, it must be configured for building on specific hosts for specific target platforms. Installation on UNIX systems is performed by the following steps: 
    cd acro
    ./configure
    make
    make executables
This will configure the software for your machine and compile the Acro library (see the Acro User Guide for more details concerning configuration options and the details of the configuration process).

Acro can also be built with the following custom configure/build process: 

    cd acro
    ./setup configure build
The setup command performs the following actions:
  1. Pipe the configuration output into acro/test/config.out and generate a test/config.xml summary file;

  2. Pipe the build output into acro/test/build.out and generate a test/build.xml summary file (this includes builds of Acro executables and test executables).
The XML files generated in the acro/test directory provide a quick reference to confirm that the software was configured and built properly. The use of setup can be extended to include configuration options and to perform tests. For example, the following configures using MPI and performs tests of various Acro packages: 
    cd acro
    ./setup configure --with-mpi  build  smoke

Online Resources

The development of PICO is supported by a variety of online software development tools, which may be of use in setting up and executing PICO software:
  1. Bugzilla - online bug reporting

  2. Mailman - mailing list management

  3. Bonsai - a query interface to CVS

The following mailing lists are used to manage Acro:

  1. acro-announce@software.sandia.gov - general announcements

  2. acro-bugs@software.sandia.gov - bug reports

  3. acro-checkins@software.sandia.gov - CVS checking reports

  4. acro-developers@software.sandia.gov - a developers mailing list

  5. acro-help@software.sandia.gov - a help list

  6. acro-misc-checkins@software.sandia.gov - CVS checking reports for miscellaneous acro components

  7. acro-users@software.sandia.gov - a general users' mailing list

The mailman server also includes the following PICO-specific mailing lists:
  1. PICO-announce@software.sandia.gov - general announcements

  2. PICO-checkins@software.sandia.gov - CVS checking reports

  3. PICO-developers@software.sandia.gov - a developers mailing list

  4. PICO-users@software.sandia.gov - a general users' mailing list
